agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position

report.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in ECL2/TM5 pockets while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions. A negative charge at position 6 will disrupt off-target electrostatic complementarity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis is updated to emphasize the importance of disrupting cons

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is updated to emphasize the importance of disrupting conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Selectivity is the primary failure mode, and future iterations should prioritize mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on these positions allows for targeted mutation to disrupt conserved pocket contacts.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets without affecting the core pharmacophore (FWKT7-10) or disulfide bonds (Cys3/Cys14).
